description_long: This object is a historical cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four metal-rimmed wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark metal, featuring decorative rings and a flared muzzle. The carriage is constructed from thick, dark brown wooden planks, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and includes two large side wheels and two smaller front wheels for mobility. Metal rings and fasteners are attached to the carriage, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, typical of artillery pieces used in historical urban fortifications or naval settings. The object exudes a sense of weight and durability, with visible wear and texture on both the wood and metal surfaces.
description: A historical cannon with a long metal barrel mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four metal-rimmed wheels and reinforced fittings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared muzzle of the cannon barrel and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the metal fasteners and wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden carriage and the alignment of the wheels, with the barrel extending forward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the length of the barrel and the carriage structure.
